q: Which class of measurement error is caused by human mistakes such as misreading instrument dials or recording incorrect data?
What type of instrumental error is caused by inherent shortcomings in the instrument itself, such as friction in bearings or weakened springs?
Errors that remain after all systematic and gross errors have been accounted for, characterized by small, unpredictable variations, are called:
How close a measured value is to the true or accepted value of the quantity being measured is defined as:
The degree of consistency or reproducibility of a set of independent measurements under unchanged conditions is defined as:
The smallest change in the measured quantity to which an instrument can respond is called the:
What type of meter movement utilizes a permanent magnet and a rotating coil to measure direct current, offering highly linear scaling?
Which of the following meters can measure both AC and DC quantities without any internal rectifying circuits?
What is the primary function of the controlling (or restoring) torque in an analog indicating instrument?
a: To accelerate the pointer toward full-scale deflection
b: To oppose the deflecting torque and bring the pointer to a steady-state rest position
c: To dampen out the mechanical vibrations of the pointer
d: To calibrate the scale for zero-adjustments

q: The three essential forces or torques required for the correct operation of any analog indicating instrument are:
What is the most efficient and widely used method of damping in PMMC-type indicating instruments?
Why is eddy current damping highly preferred in moving-coil instruments but cannot be used in moving-iron instruments?
a: Because moving-iron instruments lack a moving metallic former to generate eddy currents
b: Because the permanent magnet required for damping would distort the weak operating field of moving-iron instruments
c: Because moving-iron instruments operate purely on electrostatic fields
d: Because moving-iron scales are highly non-linear

q: Which damping method is typically employed in electrostatic voltmeters where operating magnetic fields are completely absent?
b: Fluid friction or Air friction damping

q: An ammeter is converted from a basic d'Arsonval movement by connecting a shunt resistor in what configuration?
a: In series with the meter movement
b: In parallel with the meter movement
d: In the feedback loop of the operational amplifier

q: A voltmeter is converted from a basic d'Arsonval movement by connecting a multiplier resistor in what configuration?
a: In parallel with the meter movement
b: In series with the meter movement

q: The loading effect of a voltmeter is minimized when the meter's internal resistance is:
c: Exactly equal to the source resistance

q: The insertion loss of an ammeter is minimized when the meter's internal resistance is:
c: Exactly equal to the load resistance

q: What is the sensitivity rating of a voltmeter, usually expressed in units of Ohms-per-Volt?
a: The ratio of input voltage to full-scale current
b: The reciprocal of the full-scale deflection current of the meter movement
c: The ratio of full-scale voltage to scale divisions
d: The product of scale divisions and internal resistance

q: Which of the following measurements is a common laboratory multimeter generally UNABLE to perform directly?
What specialized insulation-testing instrument is used to measure extremely high resistances, such as the insulation of cables?
Which DC bridge is specifically designed to measure extremely low values of resistance, typically below 1 Ohm, by eliminating contact and lead resistance?
A classic Wheatstone bridge is most commonly used to measure:
What is the balance condition for a standard four-arm resistive Wheatstone bridge containing arms R1, R2, R3, and R4?
Which AC bridge is used to measure medium-Q inductors (Q-factor between 1 and 10) by comparing them with a standard capacitor?
Which AC bridge is specifically modified and optimized for measuring high-Q inductors (Q-factor greater than 10)?
Which AC bridge is used for the precise measurement of capacitance, dielectric loss, and dissipation factor of capacitors under high-voltage conditions?
Which AC bridge is widely used as a frequency-determining element in audio oscillators and can also be used to measure frequency?
The Anderson bridge is a six-branch modification of the Maxwell bridge designed specifically to measure:
a: Mutual inductance in terms of known coupling
b: Self-inductance in terms of standard capacitance and resistance
d: Extremely low values of contact resistance

q: Which AC bridge is most useful for comparing the capacitances of air-dielectric capacitors?
Which bridge is used to determine self-inductance when mutual inductance is known?
What is the primary function of the sweep generator in an analog oscilloscope?
a: To amplify the vertical input signal
b: To produce a linear sawtooth waveform for horizontal deflection of the electron beam
c: To focus the electron beam onto the CRT phosphor screen
d: To control the intensity of the trace

q: In an oscilloscope, the vertical amplifier determines which of the following performance parameters?
a: The sweep rate of the horizontal trace
b: The bandwidth and input sensitivity of the oscilloscope
c: The intensity of the electron beam grid

q: What is the function of the delay line in the vertical deflection channel of an analog oscilloscope?
a: To delay the horizontal trigger until the sweep generator starts
b: To delay the vertical signal slightly so that the leading edge of the input wave can be observed on the sweep
c: To filter out high-frequency noise from the input signal

q: In a digital storage oscilloscope (DSO), real-time sampling is defined as a method where:
a: Data points are reconstructed from multiple successive cycles of a periodic wave
b: The entire waveform is captured from a single-shot event during one continuous trigger run
c: The sample rate is dynamically adjusted to match the vertical amplifier gain
d: Analog-to-digital conversion occurs before the input attenuator

q: What DSO sampling technique reconstructs a high-frequency periodic waveform by taking samples at different points over multiple successive cycles?
Which instrument is used to measure the amplitude of signal components as a function of frequency in the frequency domain?
A bolometer is a specialized measuring instrument that operates based on which principle?
a: The change in magnetic reluctance of a core under stress
b: The change in electrical resistance of a device when heated by RF or microwave power
c: The generation of a thermoelectric voltage at a bimetallic junction
d: The piezoelectric voltage generated by ultrasonic waves

q: What instrument is used to measure the rotational speed of machinery by illuminating it with light flashing at a regular, adjustable frequency?
Which standard digital interface bus is historically known as the most important standard interface for programmable electronic instrumentation systems?
How many active signal and control lines are defined in the standard 24-pin IEEE-488 (GPIB) connector?
The vertical deflection defocusing effect in CRTs represents:
a: A change in vertical sweep frequency with voltage
b: An enlargement of the electron beam spot size as it is deflected away from the screen center axis
c: A phase shift in the vertical amplifier

q: Which sensor converts high-frequency AC electrical signals or microwave power into heat, measuring its temperature rise with a thermocouple?
The sensitivity of a d'Arsonval galvanometer movement is directly proportional to which of the following physical factors?
b: The magnetic flux density and the area of the coil
c: The weight of the pointer assembly
d: The resistance of the coil wire

q: The critical damping resistance (CDRX) of a galvanometer is defined as the external resistance connected across its terminals to:
b: Achieve critical damping, bringing the pointer to rest in the shortest time without oscillation
c: Match the input impedance of the bridge circuit

q: Which type of instrument uses a heating element and a thermocouple to measure the RMS value of both AC and DC current, independent of the waveform shape?
The parallax error in an analog meter is classified under which error category?
b: Observational error (a class of systematic errors)

q: To minimize parallax error when reading an analog meter, high-precision instruments are equipped with:
b: A mirrored scale and a knife-edge pointer

q: Which type of instrument has its deflection torque proportional to the square of the voltage, making it highly valuable as a true RMS AC voltmeter?
The scale of a moving-iron or electrostatic instrument is generally:
Why is the scale of a PMMC instrument uniformly linear?
a: Because the deflecting torque is proportional to the square of the current
b: Because the deflecting torque is directly proportional to the current
c: Because it uses hairsprings for controlling torque
d: Because eddy currents neutralize the non-linear magnetic fields

q: In a dual-trace oscilloscope, 'Alternate' mode is used to display two channels by:
a: Switching rapidly between the two channels during a single sweep
b: Displaying Channel 1 for a complete sweep, and then Channel 2 for the next complete sweep
c: Combining both channel signals into a single adder circuit
d: Using two independent electron guns inside the CRT

q: In a dual-trace oscilloscope, 'Chop' mode is used to display two channels by:
a: Displaying each channel on alternate sweeps
b: Switching at a high frequency between the two channels during a single sweep

q: At what signal frequencies is 'Chop' mode preferred over 'Alternate' mode in a dual-trace oscilloscope?
At what signal frequencies is 'Alternate' mode preferred over 'Chop' mode in a dual-trace oscilloscope?
The frequency response of a standard oscilloscope probe (such as a 10x passive probe) is compensated by adjusting which component?
a: A series resistor inside the probe tip
b: A variable capacitor connected in parallel with the probe's attenuation resistor

q: What happens when an oscilloscope probe is under-compensated?
a: The displayed square wave has a sharp overshoot on the leading edges
b: The displayed square wave has rounded, sloped leading edges
c: The trace disappears from the screen
d: The input impedance of the oscilloscope drops to zero

q: What happens when an oscilloscope probe is over-compensated?
a: The displayed square wave has rounded leading edges
b: The displayed square wave has a sharp spike or overshoot on the leading edges
d: The horizontal sweep rate is decelerated

q: Which of the following is the primary purpose of using a 10x passive probe with an oscilloscope?
a: To increase the vertical sensitivity of the oscilloscope
b: To increase the input impedance and reduce the capacitive loading effect on the circuit under test
c: To filter out the horizontal sweep frequency
d: To protect the user from low-voltage static charges

q: What is the typical input impedance of a standard analog oscilloscope vertical input channel without any probe connected?
The Wagner ground connection is used in AC bridges to accomplish which of the following?
a: To increase the sensitivity of the detector
b: To eliminate the effects of stray capacitances to ground
c: To protect the operator from high AC voltages
d: To balance the bridge under DC conditions

q: What type of detector is typically used for balancing AC bridges at audio frequencies (20 Hz to 20 kHz)?
b: Headphones or an AC electronic voltmeter

q: Which instrument uses a magnetic deflection system and is primarily designed to plot the dynamic V-I characteristics of semiconductor devices?
What parameter is defined as the ratio of change in the output of an instrument to the change in input value after steady-state has been reached?
The electrostatic shielding of an AC bridge is achieved by enclosing the components inside:
a: A thick sheet of ferromagnetic steel
b: A grounded Faraday cage made of a highly conducting material like copper

q: Which error type can be mathematically evaluated using statistical methods such as the arithmetic mean and standard deviation?

a: Provide highly repeatable measurements over a long duration
b: Reproduce the input signal wave shape at its output with minimal distortion

a: The voltage range where the safety fuse opens
b: The maximum range of input values for which there is no output response

d: The time constant of the damping system

a: To generate the horizontal ramp voltage
b: To convert the analog input signal into a digital format for storage in memory
c: To calibrate the vertical gain automatically
d: To protect the input channel from overvoltages
